text.title: Competitive Positioning Worked Examples
text.summary: Two complete positioning examples showing alternatives, unique attributes, value translation, best-fit customer, and six-clause statements.

example.note: Solo project manager positioned against complex tools.
example.code:
Alternatives: Asana/Monday (feature-rich, complex), Trello (simple but limited), Spreadsheets (free but painful). Unique attributes: designed for solo creators, no team features (simpler), built-in time tracking. Time-to-copy: 6-12 quarters (teams at Asana have no product incentive to simplify — that would cannibalize team plans). Value: get organized in 5 minutes, not 5 hours. Best-fit: solo freelancers overwhelmed by big tools (trigger: just bounced from Asana free trial; WTP: $12/mo). Market category: personal project manager (existing subcategory, not new category).
Positioning statement: For solo freelancers Who need to track projects without complexity SoloBoard is a personal project manager That gets you organized in 5 minutes Unlike Asana and Monday We deliberately have no team features.

example.note: Typical "better" positioning that fails the lint check.
example.code: "SoloBoard is the easiest and most powerful project manager for modern teams." FAILS: "easiest" and "most powerful" are unsupported superlatives. FAILS: "modern teams" is not a best-fit customer — it excludes nobody. FAILS: missing Unlike clause.

example.note: Indie writer platform positioned against subscriber-based pricing.
example.code:
Alternatives: Mailchimp (all-in-one, expensive at scale), ConvertKit (creator-focused), Substack (newsletter-native). Unique attributes: flat pricing (no per-subscriber fees), focused on <10k subscribers, native Ghost/WordPress integration. Time-to-copy: 2-4 quarters (Mailchimp changing pricing model requires board-level decision; ConvertKit has subscriber-based revenue dependency). Value: grow without pricing anxiety (measurable: no bill increase when subscriber count doubles). Best-fit: indie writers and bloggers building an audience (trigger: hit 1k subscribers on Mailchimp, saw next month's bill; WTP: $29/mo flat).
Positioning statement: For indie writers growing their newsletter Who worry about costs as they scale WriterMail is email marketing That stays affordable forever Unlike Mailchimp and ConvertKit We charge flat fees, not per-subscriber.

example.note: 2x2 map to visualize gaps. Use mermaid-cli to render as SVG for stakeholder review.
example.code:
Dimensions: X-axis: Price (low -> high) Y-axis: Simplicity (complex -> simple)
Position us in the "low price + simple" quadrant. Competitors Asana/Monday: high price + complex. Trello: low price + complex (more features than it looks). Gap: low price + simple -- that is our position.
Mermaid render: mmdc -i positioning-map.mmd -o positioning-map.svg
